danger_signals.visual_form: Grotesque creature designs; biomechanical/organic hybrid aesthetics
danger_signals.meaning: Extraterrestrial threat to Earth; invasion state
Corruption of human form; environmental contamination/alien infection

danger_signals.visual_form: Police officers with pig-like features; 'LARD' emblazoned uniforms
danger_signals.meaning: Subversion of authority; humans transformed/corrupted by alien influence

danger_signals.visual_form: Shadowed environments requiring night vision goggles
danger_signals.meaning: Concealment of threats; danger lurking unseen

danger_signals.visual_form: Breakable props and interactive objects throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat zone; environmental hazard and instability

safety_signals.visual_form: Interactive switches that illuminate dark areas
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency to control visibility; tactical advantage and safety
Environmental life support; remnants of functional civilization

safety_signals.visual_form: Air ducts, back doors, sewers as alternate routes
safety_signals.meaning: Escape routes and tactical positioning options

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Transformed into Pig Cops (humanoid with porcine features); indicates corruption/subjugation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Police uniforms, urban institutional structures (streets, buildings)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple alien enemy types with biomechanical characteristics; grotesque morphologies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Alien contamination visible through mutated humans and environmental corruption
Strip clubs, shops (Shop-N-Bag), commercial signage; everyday urban spaces converted to battlegrounds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mix of recognizable Earth environments (Hollywood, urban spaces) occupied by grotesque alien creatures and mutated humans
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively hostile; Pig Cops enforce alien occupation; normal human NPCs absent or corrupted

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Destroyed environments; breakable props indicating combat damage; civilian infrastructure (shops, streets) repurposed as war zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No functioning civilian structures; all encounters are hostile; authority figures transformed into enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapons and supplies scattered throughout; health items available; multiple paths suggesting human defensive planning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player equipped with human-origin weapons (firearms, conventional explosives); survival mechanics indicate ongoing conflict

environmental_storytelling.example: Hollywood locations (iconic Earth settings) converted to alien battlegrounds; communicates global scale of invasion
LAPD transformed into Pig Cops with grotesque features; shows complete breakdown of human authority structures
Strip clubs, shops, transit systems repurposed as combat arenas; indicates invasion's penetration into everyday life
Medkits, ammo, and supplies hidden throughout; suggests remnants of human civilization and survival necessity
Air ducts, sewers, back doors visible and explorable; presents human infrastructure as escape/infiltration network

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Actively hostile; pursuing player; indicate complete subjugation of authority
Aggressive combat; varied attack patterns; indicate occupying force intensity
Armed combat; exploration; weapon acquisition; indicates human capacity to fight back
Destructible on player action; provide resources when damaged; indicate environmental instability

notes: Duke Nukem 3D communicates world-state primarily through enemy transformation (civilian LAPD → Pig Cops indicates authority corruption), environmental repurposing (civilian spaces as war zones), and visual grotesquerie of aliens. The game uses interactivity and resource placement to suggest human survival rather than explicit narrative exposition. Urban landmarks serve as visual anchors for the invasion's global scope. Safety signals emerge through player agency (light switches, alternate routes, health pickups) rather than protected spaces, emphasizing active resistance over sanctuary.
